blurbs.root:
```xml
<intent>what is the API version and available endpoints</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns service metadata and health status at root path only</gotchas>
```
blurbs.live:
```xml
<intent>check if the service is running and responsive</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>liveness probe only indicates the process is alive, not that it is ready to serve traffic—use readiness probe for that distinction</gotchas>
```
blurbs.ready:
```xml
<intent>check if the service is ready to handle requests</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>readiness probe; indicates service readiness state rather than general liveness; used for load balancer routing decisions</gotchas>
```
blurbs.list_alerts:
```xml
<intent>what recent alerts or state changes have occurred, optionally filtered by asset or class</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns recent historical alerts, not a live stream; filtered by optional asset/class parameters and time window via since parameter</gotchas>
```
blurbs.list_assets:
```xml
<intent>what assets are currently available for trading</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>results can be filtered by class and peg optional parameters; returns only active assets</gotchas>
```
blurbs.detail_by_mint:
```xml
<intent>retrieve a single digital asset details by its mint address</intent>
<required>mint (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns one specific asset for an exact mint match; use list endpoints for searching or filtering multiple assets</gotchas>
```
blurbs.state_by_mint:
```xml
<intent>what is the current peg state for a specific asset mint address</intent>
<required>mint (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns state for a single mint only; requires valid mint address format</gotchas>
```
blurbs.asset_detail:
```xml
<intent>What are the details of a specific asset by its ticker symbol</intent>
<required>symbol (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>Returns a single asset record; use list endpoint for multiple assets or filtering</gotchas>
```
blurbs.history:
```xml
<intent>what is the historical discount price progression for a specific asset over time</intent>
<required>symbol (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns time-series data; optional bucket, from, to, and limit parameters control granularity, date range, and result count</gotchas>
```
blurbs.state:
<intent>what is the current peg state of an asset</intent>
<required>symbol (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns real-time peg state; use symbol parameter to specify which asset to query</gotchas>
blurbs.index:
```xml
<intent>show me recent audit receipts or audit logs</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>supports filtering by limit, state, and optionally excluding pegged items; returns a snapshot of recent entries, not a live stream</gotchas>
```
blurbs.csv:
```xml
<intent>export audit receipts to a CSV file for a date range</intent>
<required>from (string), to (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>optional asset parameter filters results by specific asset; date range is required and determines scope of exported records</gotchas>
```
blurbs.audit_detail:
```xml
<intent>retrieve details about a specific audit alert or receipt by its ID</intent>
<required>alert_id (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns raw Response object allowing distinct headers and body per status code, not standard JSON wrapper; implements four-state discrimination per ADR-0006</gotchas>
```
blurbs.onchain:
```xml
<intent>what is the on-chain commit pointer for a specific alert</intent>
<required>alert_id (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns on-chain commit reference data specific to a single alert by its identifier</gotchas>
```
blurbs.replay_bundle:
```xml
<intent>download the replay bundle for a specific alert to review its details and history</intent>
<required>alert_id (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>retrieves a bundled archive of replay data for a single alert; requires knowing the specific alert_id</gotchas>
```
blurbs.logout:
<intent>how do I log out and revoke my session token</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>accepts expired tokens, allowing logout from stale sessions</gotchas>
blurbs.consume_magic:
```xml
<intent>redeem a magic link nonce and get a session token after clicking a magic link</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>atomically consumes nonce and returns telegram_id in one operation; concurrent requests cannot both succeed; returns 7-day JWT on success</gotchas>
```
blurbs.mint_magic:
```xml
<intent>create a magic link nonce for web authentication when the bot initiates a user login</intent>
<required>x-internal-secret (string)</required>
<auth>none; validates via x-internal-secret header constant-time comparison</auth>
<gotchas>internal endpoint only; called by bot on /web command; nonce created here but JWT issued later on consume</gotchas>
```
blurbs.login:
```xml
<intent>log in using telegram verified login widget payload and get a session jwt</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>payload must include valid HMAC signature verified with bot token; issues 7-day session JWT not permanent credentials</gotchas>
```
blurbs.calibration_health:
```xml
<intent>check the calibration health and performance statistics for assets, comparing 95th percentile values against expected bands over an 8-day period</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns aggregated per-asset calibration metrics over a fixed 8-day window; this is a snapshot of historical self-monitoring data, not real-time monitoring</gotchas>
```
blurbs.me:
```xml
<intent>what is my user profile and email digest preferences</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>returns current authenticated user only; requires valid JWT token in request</gotchas>
```
blurbs.list_my_alerts:
```xml
<intent>show me the alerts sent to my subscriptions, sorted by newest first</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>supports optional filtering by asset, pagination limit (1–500), and time cutoff via since parameter</gotchas>
```
blurbs.preferences:
```xml
<intent>update my language preference and email digest frequency settings</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>omitted fields remain unchanged; locale values are restricted to en or pt-BR</gotchas>
```
blurbs.list_subs:
```xml
<intent>show my alert subscriptions</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>returns active subscriptions first in the list</gotchas>
```
blurbs.create_sub:
```xml
<intent>how do I set up an alert subscription to notify me when an asset reaches a specific bits-per-second threshold</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>operation is idempotent on asset, threshold, and channel combination; can re-activate a previously created subscription</gotchas>
```
blurbs.delete_sub:
```xml
<intent>remove or delete a subscription to an asset</intent>
<required>asset (string)</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>soft-delete by default unless hard=true; only_id parameter scopes deletion to a single subscription row instead of all rows for the user+asset pair</gotchas>
```
blurbs.patch_sub:
```xml
<intent>update my subscription settings like threshold or active state for a specific asset</intent>
<required>asset (string)</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>only_id parameter lets you target a single subscription row instead of all subscriptions for that asset</gotchas>
```
blurbs.list_webhooks:
```xml
<intent>what webhook subscriptions do I have set up</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>returns only the authenticated caller's own webhooks, not a global list</gotchas>
```
blurbs.create_webhook:
```xml
<intent>set up a webhook to receive signed notifications when an asset changes</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>webhook URL must pass SSRF and length validation; operation is idempotent on URL and asset pair; uses Ed25519 signatures for message authentication</gotchas>
```
blurbs.delete_webhook:
```xml
<intent>disable or deactivate my webhook without removing its history</intent>
<required>id (string)</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>soft-delete only: deactivates webhook (is_active=false) but preserves delivery history rather than permanent deletion</gotchas>
```
blurbs.patch_webhook:
```xml
<intent>update my webhook settings while keeping its delivery history and ID</intent>
<required>id (string)</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>partial update preserves webhook ID and audit trail; differs from delete-recreate which orphans history under a new ID</gotchas>
```
blurbs.list_deliveries:
```xml
<intent>show me the delivery history and status of all webhook attempts sent to this subscription</intent>
<required>id (string)</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>includes both production deliveries and test/replay attempts; ordered newest first; shows successes, failures, and errors in a single audit trail</gotchas>
```
blurbs.replay_webhook:
```xml
<intent>replay webhook deliveries that failed and are stuck in the dead-letter queue</intent>
<required>id (string)</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>successful replays remain in dead-letter table for audit; receiver deduplicates via x-pegana-event-id header; optional since and limit parameters filter the window of rows to retry</gotchas>
```
blurbs.test_webhook:
```xml
<intent>send a test webhook payload to my registered webhook URL to verify it's working</intent>
<required>id (string)</required>
<auth>required; telegram_jwt</auth>
<gotchas>synthetic payload includes x-pegana-test header; no automatic retries unlike production webhooks; operator-triggered one-shot only</gotchas>
```
blurbs.webhook_ips:
```xml
<intent>what IP addresses should I allowlist for webhooks from this service</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns static egress IPs configured via environment variable; useful for firewall rules but not a real-time status endpoint</gotchas>
```
blurbs.webhook_keys:
```xml
<intent>what are the current public keys for verifying webhook signatures</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns both primary and secondary keys during rotation periods to allow pre-trust of incoming keys before cutover</gotchas>
```
blurbs.current:
```xml
<intent>what is the current active methodology version</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns the currently active version only, not historical versions or all available versions</gotchas>
```
blurbs.peg_feed:
```xml
<intent>get the current peg signals for all active assets organized by their mint address</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns a batch snapshot of all active assets rather than a single asset; keyed by mint identifier</gotchas>
```
blurbs.peg_feed_by_mint:
```xml
<intent>get the peg signal or price feed data for a specific cryptocurrency asset identified by its mint address</intent>
<required>mint (string)</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>retrieves data for a single mint only; use to look up one asset's peg status rather than querying multiple assets or the full feed</gotchas>
```
blurbs.summary:
```xml
<intent>what are the overall aggregate counters and delivery health statistics</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>returns summary aggregates rather than detailed per-item breakdowns or time-series data</gotchas>
```
blurbs.ws_handler:
```xml
<intent>get a live real-time stream of peg state updates via WebSocket</intent>
<required>none</required>
<auth>none</auth>
<gotchas>upgrades HTTP connection to WebSocket protocol; server pushes updates and heartbeats continuously; client can subscribe/unsubscribe to specific assets; requires allowlisted Origin header for browsers</gotchas>
```
